out_of_scope_note: Psycholinguistics / experimental pragmatics demonstration that lay readers infer causality from natural-language correlational statements (e.g. 'X is associated with Y'). Pure language-comprehension survey with no neural recording, no computational model of cognition, and no theoretical framework from the computational-neuroscience canon (Bayesian observer, predictive coding, drift-diffusion, etc.). Falls under the 'social-science / language survey without neural or computational-brain content' out-of-scope category.
